deep_dive.title: iDiff: Interpretable Difference-aware Framework for Pairwise Image Quality Assessment
deep_dive.abstract: Pairwise image quality assessment (IQA) in professional photography requires a model not only to identify the preferred image between two candidates, but also to provide convincing and image-grounded reasoning. In the NTIRE 2026 RAIM challenge, this requirement is further emphasized by jointly evaluating preference prediction and rationale generation. To address this task, we propose iDiff, an Interpretable Difference-aware framework for pairwise image quality assessment. Our method adopts a dual-branch design consisting of an Answer Model and a Thinking Model. The Answer Model performs robust preference prediction by explicitly decomposing each sample into left/right global and local views, followed by content-aware specialization for person and scene images and ensemble-based aggregation across backbones. The Thinking Model focuses on rationale generation and is progressively enhanced with expert-style templates, multi-source quality features, and answer-aware supervision conditioned on the Answer Model prediction. In this way, iDiff jointly models discriminative decision making and structured explanation, improving both robustness and interpretability. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework on both accuracy and reasoning-quality metrics. Our method achieved first place in the NTIRE 2026 RAIM challenge, showing the effectiveness of integrating explicit difference modeling with structured multimodal reasoning for pairwise IQA.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: pairwise IQA with image-grounded reasoning and explanation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 成对图像质量评估，提供基于图像的推理和解释
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 成对图像质量评估，提供基于图像的推理和解释
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 成对图像质量评估，提供基于图像的推理和解释
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: iDiff targets pairwise IQA in professional photography, requiring both preference prediction and convincing reasoning. It introduces a dual-branch framework with an Answer Model and a Thinking Model for decomposed global/local views and content-aware specialization, demonstrated on the NTIRE 2026 RAIM challenge.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 专业摄影中的成对图像质量评估不仅需要识别偏好图像，还需提供有说服力的推理。本文提出iDiff，一个可解释的差异感知框架，采用双分支设计（答案模型和思维模型），通过显式分解左右全局和局部视图并引入内容感知专业化，同时完成偏好预测和理由生成。在NTIRE 2026 RAIM挑战中验证了其有效性，为可解释的图像质量评估提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 专业摄影中的成对图像质量评估不仅需要识别偏好图像，还需提供有说服力的推理。本文提出iDiff，一个可解释的差异感知框架，采用双分支设计（答案模型和思维模型），通过显式分解左右全局和局部视图并引入内容感知专业化，同时完成偏好预测和理由生成。在NTIRE 2026 RAIM挑战中验证了其有效性，为可解释的图像质量评估提供了新方案。

deep_dive.title: DynT2I-Eval: A Dynamic Evaluation Framework for Text-to-Image Models
deep_dive.abstract: Existing text-to-image (T2I) benchmarks largely rely on fixed prompt sets, leaving them vulnerable to overfitting and benchmark contamination once publicly released and repeatedly reused. In this work, we propose DynT2I-Eval, a fully automated dynamic evaluation framework for T2I models. It constructs a structured visual semantic space from long-form descriptions, decomposing prompts into controllable dimensions (e.g., subject, logical constraint, environment, and composition). This enables the continuous generation of fresh prompts via task-specific spaces and difficulty-aware sampling. DynT2I-Eval evaluates model performance across text alignment, perceptual quality, and aesthetics. Heterogeneous outputs are unified into prompt-conditioned pairwise comparisons, allowing a dynamic scheduler, micro-batch aggregation, and weighted Bayesian updates to maintain a stable online leaderboard despite changing prompt distributions and model injection. Experiments with independently sampled prompt streams demonstrate that continually refreshed prompts provide a robust evaluation protocol, reducing the impact of prompt-set-specific tuning. Simulations and ablations further confirm that the proposed ranking framework achieves a strong balance among cold-start convergence, late-entry discovery, and long-run ranking fidelity.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evaluates text-to-image models on perceptual quality and aesthetics, directly assessing image quality through a dynamic benchmark.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在感知质量和美学维度上评估文生图模型，直接评价图像质量。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在感知质量和美学维度上评估文生图模型，直接评价图像质量。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在感知质量和美学维度上评估文生图模型，直接评价图像质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DynT2I-Eval introduces a dynamic benchmark that generates fresh prompts from a structured semantic space to evaluate text-to-image models on alignment, perceptual quality, and aesthetics, preventing overfitting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有文生图评估基准多采用固定提示，面临过拟合与基准污染风险。DynT2I-Eval提出基于结构化视觉语义空间的动态评估框架，将长文本描述分解为可控维度，持续生成新鲜提示，并通过提示条件成对比较统一异构输出，评估模型的文本对齐、感知质量与美学表现。实验表明，该框架能够有效分离模型能力，提供更细粒度且不易被利用的评估结果，为文生图领域提供了更有生命力的评测方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有文生图评估基准多采用固定提示，面临过拟合与基准污染风险。DynT2I-Eval提出基于结构化视觉语义空间的动态评估框架，将长文本描述分解为可控维度，持续生成新鲜提示，并通过提示条件成对比较统一异构输出，评估模型的文本对齐、感知质量与美学表现。实验表明，该框架能够有效分离模型能力，提供更细粒度且不易被利用的评估结果，为文生图领域提供了更有生命力的评测方案。

deep_dive.title: Bringing Multimodal Large Language Models to Infrared-Visible Image Fusion Quality Assessment
deep_dive.abstract: Infrared-Visible image fusion (IVIF) aims to integrate thermal information and detailed spatial structures into a single fused image to enhance perception. However, existing evaluation approaches tend to over-optimize both hand-crafted no-reference statistics and full-reference metrics that treat the source images as pseudo ground truths. Recent IVIF reward-modelling efforts learn from human ratings but use scalar regression on aggregated scores, neither leveraging the reasoning of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) nor encoding per-image perceptual ambiguity in their supervision, but naively introducing MLLMs with discrete one-hot supervision likewise collapses fused images of similar quality into different rating levels. To address this, we introduce FuScore, which utilizes an MLLM to mimic human visual perception by producing continuous quality score, rather than discrete level predictions, enabling fine-grained discrimination among fused images of similar quality. We exploit the agreement among four IVIF-specific sub-dimensions to construct a per-image soft label whose sharpness reflects how consensual the overall judgment is. We further introduce a tripartite objective combining per-image distributional supervision, within-source-pair Thurstone fidelity for method-level ordering, and cross-source-pair Thurstone fidelity for scene-level ordering across scenes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FuScore achieves state-of-the-art correlation with human visual preferences.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Applies MLLMs to reason about fusion image quality, addressing limitations of scalar regression and naive MLLM use.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 应用多模态大语言模型推理融合图像质量，解决标量回归和朴素MLLM使用的局限性。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 应用多模态大语言模型推理融合图像质量，解决标量回归和朴素MLLM使用的局限性。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 应用多模态大语言模型推理融合图像质量，解决标量回归和朴素MLLM使用的局限性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses limitations in infrared-visible image fusion quality assessment by leveraging MLLMs for reasoning and capturing perceptual ambiguity. The method moves beyond scalar regression to generate language-based quality evaluations, enabling more human-aligned and explainable IQA.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前红外-可见光图像融合质量评估方法过度依赖手工统计量或将源图像视为伪真值，而基于奖励建模的方法使用标量回归，未利用多模态大语言模型的推理能力。本文提出引入MLLMs进行IVIF质量推理，克服了标量回归和简单离散监督的局限，能捕捉每张图像的感知模糊性并生成文本解释。该方法实现了更符合人类感知、可解释的图像融合质量评价。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前红外-可见光图像融合质量评估方法过度依赖手工统计量或将源图像视为伪真值，而基于奖励建模的方法使用标量回归，未利用多模态大语言模型的推理能力。本文提出引入MLLMs进行IVIF质量推理，克服了标量回归和简单离散监督的局限，能捕捉每张图像的感知模糊性并生成文本解释。该方法实现了更符合人类感知、可解释的图像融合质量评价。

deep_dive.abstract: Infrared-Visible image fusion (IVIF) aims to integrate thermal information and detailed spatial structures into a single fused image to enhance perception. However, existing evaluation approaches tend to over-optimize both hand-crafted no-reference statistics and full-reference metrics that treat the source images as pseudo ground truths. We further introduce a tripartite objective combining per-image distributional supervision, within-source-pair Thurstone fidelity for method-level ordering, and cross-source-pair Thurstone fidelity for scene-level ordering across scenes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FuScore achieves state-of-the-art correlation with human visual preferences.

deep_dive.title: APEX: Assumption-free Projection-based Embedding eXamination Metric for Image Quality Assessment
deep_dive.abstract: As generative models achieve unprecedented visual quality, the gold standard for image evaluation remains traditional feature-distribution metrics (e.g., FID). However, these metrics are provably hindered by the closed-vocabulary bottleneck of outdated features and the assumptive bias of rigid parametric formulations. Recent alternatives exploit modern backbones to solve the feature bottleneck, yet continue to suffer from parametric limitations. To close this gap, we introduce APEX (Assumption-free Projection-based Embedding eXamination), a novel evaluation framework leveraging the Sliced Wasserstein Distance as a mathematically grounded, assumption-free similarity measure. APEX inherits effective scalability to high-dimensional spaces, as we prove with theoretical and empirical evidences. Moreover, APEX is embedding-agnostic and uses two open-vocabulary foundation models, CLIP and DINOv2, as feature extractors. Benchmarking APEX against established baselines reveals superior robustness to visual degradations. Additionally, we show that APEX metrics exhibit intra- and cross-dataset stability, ensuring highly stable evaluations on out-of-domain datasets.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a new image quality assessment metric using sliced Wasserstein distance for evaluating generative models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于切片Wasserstein距离的新图像质量评估指标，用于评估生成模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于切片Wasserstein距离的新图像质量评估指标，用于评估生成模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于切片Wasserstein距离的新图像质量评估指标，用于评估生成模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces APEX, a novel IQA metric that leverages the sliced Wasserstein distance to overcome the closed-vocabulary bottleneck and parametric biases of traditional distribution metrics like FID, providing a mathematically grounded and assumption-free evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统生成模型评价指标（如FID）受限于封闭词汇特征和参数化假设偏差。本文提出APEX指标，采用切片Wasserstein距离作为无假设相似性度量，利用现代骨干网络提取高维特征，实现数学严密且可扩展的图像质量评估。实验表明APEX克服了特征瓶颈和参数化限制，为图像生成质量提供了更可靠的评价。

However, these metrics are provably hindered by the closed-vocabulary bottleneck of outdated features and the assumptive bias of rigid parametric formulations. Recent alternatives exploit modern backbones to solve the feature bottleneck, yet continue to suffer from parametric limitations. To close this gap, we introduce APEX (Assumption-free Projection-based Embedding eXamination), a novel evaluation framework leveraging the Sliced Wasserstein Distance as a mathematically grounded, assumption-free similarity measure. deep_dive.title: IPAD-CLIP: Teaching CLIP to Detect Image Local Perceptual Artifacts
deep_dive.abstract: Current image quality assessment methods are heavily biased towards global distortions (e.g., noise, blur), neglecting local perceptual artifacts such as ghosting, lens flare, and moire effects. Although significant progress has been made in artifact removal, the fundamental problem of automatic artifact detection remains largely unexplored. In this paper, we formalize the Image Perceptual Artifact Detection (IPAD) task to address this gap. We contribute a benchmark dataset comprising 3,520 artifact images, including 520 real-captured and 3,000 synthetic samples, each paired with pixel-level masks across three representative artifact categories. The core challenge of IPAD lies in the localized, subtle, and semantically weak nature of these artifacts, which makes them prone to missed detection. To overcome this, we introduce IPAD-CLIP, a novel framework built upon CLIP that enhances artifact discrimination in both textual and visual spaces while preserving generalization capabilities. Our key insight is that local artifacts often exhibit strong correlations with specific semantic contexts. Accordingly, we learn artifact-aware text embeddings to explicitly model the object-artifact relationships, resulting in enhanced representations that clear differentiate between clean and artifact prompts. These text embeddings are then used as anchors to shift the visual encoder's attention from high-level semantics to subtle, low-level artifacts. Extensive experiments demonstrate that IPAD-CLIP offers a resource-efficient adaptation of CLIP for detection, significantly outperforming advanced image anomaly detection and manipulation detection methods on our benchmark. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study addressing multi-class local perceptual artifact detection in terms of both dataset and model.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: detection of local perceptual artifacts for image quality assessment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 检测局部感知伪影以评估图像质量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 检测局部感知伪影以评估图像质量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 检测局部感知伪影以评估图像质量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper formalizes the Image Perceptual Artifact Detection (IPAD) task for local distortions like ghosting and flare, proposes a CLIP-based detection method and a benchmark dataset, advancing image quality assessment by addressing neglected local artifacts.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有图像质量评价方法偏重全局失真，忽视鬼影、镜头眩光等局部感知伪影。本文正式定义图像感知伪影检测（IPAD）任务，构建包含520张真实图像和3000张合成图像及像素级掩码的数据集，并提出基于CLIP的检测方法IPAD-CLIP。实验证明该方法能有效检测三类代表性伪影，为自动伪影检测奠定基础，对提升图像质量评价的全面性具有重要意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有图像质量评价方法偏重全局失真，忽视鬼影、镜头眩光等局部感知伪影。本文正式定义图像感知伪影检测（IPAD）任务，构建包含520张真实图像和3000张合成图像及像素级掩码的数据集，并提出基于CLIP的检测方法IPAD-CLIP。实验证明该方法能有效检测三类代表性伪影，为自动伪影检测奠定基础，对提升图像质量评价的全面性具有重要意义。

deep_dive.title: SenseBench: A Benchmark for Remote Sensing Low-Level Visual Perception and Description in Large Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Low-level visual perception underpins reliable remote sensing (RS) image analysis, yet current image quality assessment (IQA) methods output uninterpretable scalar scores rather than characterizing physics-driven RS degradations, deviating markedly from the diagnostic needs of RS experts. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) present a compelling alternative by delivering language-grounded IQA, their visual priors are heavily biased toward ground-level natural images. Consequently, whether VLMs can overcome this domain gap to perceive and articulate RS artifacts remains insufficiently studied. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{SenseBench}, the first dedicated diagnostic benchmark for RS low-level visual perception and description. Driven by a physics-based hierarchical taxonomy that unifies both non-reference and reference-based paradigms, SenseBench features over 10K meticulously curated instances across 6 major and 22 fine-grained RS degradation categories. Specifically, two complementary protocols are designed for evaluation: objective low-level visual \textit{perception} and subjective diagnostic \textit{description}. Comprehensive evaluation of 29 state-of-the-art VLMs reveals not only skewed domain priors and multi-distortion collapse, but also \textit{fluency illusion} and a \textit{perception-description inversion} effect. We hope SenseBench provides a robust evaluation testbed and high-quality diagnostic data to advance the development of VLMs in RS low-level perception. Code and datasets are available \href{https://github.com/Zhong-Chenchen/SenseBench}{\textcolor{blue}{here}}.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Benchmark for VLM-based language-grounded image quality assessment in remote sensing, providing descriptions of degradations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向遥感VLM语言基础图像质量评估的基准，提供退化描述。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向遥感VLM语言基础图像质量评估的基准，提供退化描述。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向遥感VLM语言基础图像质量评估的基准，提供退化描述。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses the lack of interpretable IQA in remote sensing by introducing SenseBench, a benchmark for evaluating how well VLMs can perceive and describe low-level image degradations. It aims to shift from scalar scores to natural language explanations for image quality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前遥感图像质量评估方法仅输出不可解释的标量分数，无法满足专家诊断需求。该论文提出SenseBench，首个面向遥感低层视觉感知与描述的诊断基准，旨在评估大视觉语言模型感知和描述物理驱动的遥感图像退化的能力。通过该基准，可以推动从标量评分到自然语言解释的转变，实现语言基础的可解释图像质量评估。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前遥感图像质量评估方法仅输出不可解释的标量分数，无法满足专家诊断需求。该论文提出SenseBench，首个面向遥感低层视觉感知与描述的诊断基准，旨在评估大视觉语言模型感知和描述物理驱动的遥感图像退化的能力。通过该基准，可以推动从标量评分到自然语言解释的转变，实现语言基础的可解释图像质量评估。

deep_dive.title: SR-Prominence: A Crowdsourced Protocol and Dataset Suite for Perceptually-Weighted Super-Resolution Artifact Evaluation
deep_dive.abstract: Modern image super-resolution methods generate detailed, visually appealing results, but they often introduce visual artifacts: unnatural patterns and texture distortions that degrade perceived quality. These defects vary widely in perceptual impact--some are barely noticeable, while others are highly disturbing--yet existing detection methods treat them equally. We propose artifact prominence as an evaluative target, defined as the fraction of viewers who judge a highlighted region to contain a noticeable artifact. We design a crowdsourced annotation protocol and construct SR-Prominence, a dataset suite containing 3,935 artifact masks from DeSRA, Open Images, Urban100, and a realistic no-ground-truth Urban100-HR setting, annotated with prominence. Re-annotating DeSRA reveals that 48.2% of its in-lab binary artifacts are not noticed by a majority of viewers. Across the suite, we audit SR artifact detectors, image-quality metrics, and SR methods. We find that classical full-reference metrics, especially SSIM and DISTS, provide surprisingly strong localized prominence signals, whereas no-reference IQA methods and specialized artifact detectors often fail to generalize across datasets and reference settings. SR-Prominence is released with an objective scoring protocol that allows new metrics to be benchmarked on our suite without further crowdsourcing. Together, the data and protocols enable SR artifact evaluation to move from binary defect presence toward perceptual impact. SR-Prominence is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/imolodetskikh/sr-artifact-prominence.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: perceptually-weighted evaluation of super-resolution artifacts in images
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对图像超分辨率伪影的感知加权评估
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对图像超分辨率伪影的感知加权评估
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对图像超分辨率伪影的感知加权评估
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SR-Prominence defines artifact prominence as the fraction of viewers noticing an artifact, creating a dataset and protocol for evaluating SR artifacts with perceptual weighting. This enables more nuanced quality assessment of super-resolved images.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对超分辨率图像伪影评估忽视感知影响差异的问题，提出伪影显著度概念，定义为主观评分中注意到伪影的观看者比例。设计众包标注协议，构建包含3935个伪影掩码的SR-Prominence数据集，覆盖多种场景。实验表明，显著度加权评估能更精细区分伪影的感知严重性，为图像增强质量评估提供新指标。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对超分辨率图像伪影评估忽视感知影响差异的问题，提出伪影显著度概念，定义为主观评分中注意到伪影的观看者比例。设计众包标注协议，构建包含3935个伪影掩码的SR-Prominence数据集，覆盖多种场景。实验表明，显著度加权评估能更精细区分伪影的感知严重性，为图像增强质量评估提供新指标。

deep_dive.title: MSIQ: Moment-based Scale-Invariant Quality Measure for Single Image Super-Resolution
deep_dive.abstract: Assessing the quality of single image super-resolution (SISR) results remains an open methodological problem. Common full-reference metrics (PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS) do not explicitly evaluate the preservation of the geometric structure of images, which is critical for the correctness of scale-based reconstruction. In addition, they require the forced alignment of images to the same size (\textit{forced resizing}), which introduces an external interpolation error into the evaluation process.   This paper proposes a diagnostic scale-invariant quality measure, MSIQ (\textit{Moment-based Scale-Invariant Quality}), based on the comparison of normalized central geometric moments of two images. MSIQ enables direct comparison of images with different spatial resolutions without resizing, is mathematically deterministic (\textit{model-free}), and has an analytical form. To provide a theoretical basis for the approach, we introduce a conceptual distinction between the ability of metrics to monotonically track degradation (\textit{tracking ability}) and their geometric selectivity (\textit{geometric specificity}).   The experimental validation confirmed the stability of MSIQ under uniform scaling and, at the same time, revealed the high sensitivity of traditional metrics to the choice of interpolation method. The results show that MSIQ has pronounced geometric selectivity: the proposed measure effectively separates geometric deformations from non-geometric artifacts, in particular JPEG compression, unlike pixel-based and perceptual metrics. It is also shown that the response of MSIQ to structural perturbations remains stable across different classes of SR algorithms, including DNN models with different architectures. The proposed measure is a complementary diagnostic tool for domains where geometric fidelity has priority, in particular medical imaging and remote sensing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a scale-invariant quality measure for single image super-resolution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出单图像超分辨率的尺度不变质量评价指标
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出单图像超分辨率的尺度不变质量评价指标
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出单图像超分辨率的尺度不变质量评价指标
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: introduces MSIQ, a diagnostic scale-invariant quality measure based on normalized central geometric moments to evaluate geometric structure preservation in SISR.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有全参考指标无法显式评估几何结构保持且引入强制缩放误差的问题，提出基于归一化中心几何矩的尺度不变质量指标MSIQ。MSIQ允许直接比较不同尺寸的图像，通过矩比较反映几何结构还原程度，为超分辨率评估提供了更准确的诊断工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有全参考指标无法显式评估几何结构保持且引入强制缩放误差的问题，提出基于归一化中心几何矩的尺度不变质量指标MSIQ。MSIQ允许直接比较不同尺寸的图像，通过矩比较反映几何结构还原程度，为超分辨率评估提供了更准确的诊断工具。

deep_dive.title: Unleashing Vision Transformer Potential In Image Quality Assessment via Global-Local Adaptive Interaction
deep_dive.abstract: In the field of Blind Image Quality Assessment (BIQA), accurately predicting the perceptual quality of authentically distorted images remains highly challenging due to the diverse and complex distortions present in natural environments. Although existing methods have achieved notable accuracy, their scalability is often constrained by the high cost of subjective annotation and the limited size of available datasets. Recent advances in large-scale pre-trained vision models have introduced powerful semantic and representational capabilities, yet their application to IQA tasks is hindered by substantial computational demands and suboptimal fine-tuning efficiency. To overcome these limitations, we introduce the Global-Local Interaction Adapter (GLIA), a novel framework that effectively harnesses pre-trained Vision Transformers through a dual-stream feature extraction mechanism coupled with interactive global-local fusion. By jointly retaining global semantic information and fine-grained local details, our approach delivers superior prediction accuracy and robustness while requiring significantly fewer trainable parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks validate the effectiveness and superiority of our approach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes Global-Local Adaptive Interaction for Blind Image Quality Assessment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出全局-局部自适应交互用于盲图像质量评估
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出全局-局部自适应交互用于盲图像质量评估
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出全局-局部自适应交互用于盲图像质量评估
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: introduces a Global-Local Adaptive Interaction method to unlock Vision Transformer potential for Blind IQA, addressing annotation cost and computational demands.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对真实失真图像质量评估标注成本高、数据集小且预训练视觉模型微调效率低的问题，提出全局-局部自适应交互方法以释放Vision Transformer在盲图像质量评估中的潜力。该方法平衡了全局语义和局部细节感知，在多个IQA数据集上取得先进性能，展示了大规模预训练模型在IQA中的高效应用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对真实失真图像质量评估标注成本高、数据集小且预训练视觉模型微调效率低的问题，提出全局-局部自适应交互方法以释放Vision Transformer在盲图像质量评估中的潜力。该方法平衡了全局语义和局部细节感知，在多个IQA数据集上取得先进性能，展示了大规模预训练模型在IQA中的高效应用。

deep_dive.title: Preferences Order, Ratings Anchor: From Fused Expert Aesthetic Ground Truth to Self-Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: Pairwise preferences and pointwise ratings are the two dominant annotation protocols in image aesthetic assessment (IAA), yet existing benchmarks adopt only one, leaving their complementarity unmeasured under controlled conditions. We introduce PPaint, a matched dual-protocol benchmark in which 15 domain experts, 5 per category, annotate 150 Chinese paintings under both protocols across five aesthetic dimensions, collecting 45,900 pairwise expert judgments through a locally dense preference design alongside the matched ratings. The matched design reveals complementary strengths: preferences yield more consistent ordinal rankings, while ratings anchor the absolute score scale. Fusing both signals via two independent preference-to-score methods yields a fused expert ground truth on which the two constructions converge to nearly identical scores. The same preference-to-score principle extends to label-free VLM training. PSDistill converts VLM pairwise judgments into calibrated pseudo-scores via an Elo reference pool, and trains the same VLM with confidence-weighted ranking optimization to produce a single-pass aesthetic scorer. Trained on a single painting category, the distilled Qwen3-VL-8B improves mean SRCC from 0.504 to 0.709 across all three categories, outperforming all open-source baselines including the dedicated aesthetic model ArtiMuse and matching closed-source Gemini-3.1-Pro within 0.04 SRCC at single-pass inference cost, with cross-domain transfer further validated on APDDv2. We will release the full PPaint dataset and training code.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces a dual-protocol benchmark for image aesthetic assessment, fusing pairwise preferences and pointwise ratings to build robust ground truth for evaluating image quality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 引入双协议基准进行图像美学评估，融合成对偏好与逐点评分以构建鲁棒的图像质量评价真值。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 引入双协议基准进行图像美学评估，融合成对偏好与逐点评分以构建鲁棒的图像质量评价真值。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 引入双协议基准进行图像美学评估，融合成对偏好与逐点评分以构建鲁棒的图像质量评价真值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PPaint benchmark matches pairwise preference and pointwise ratings for image aesthetic assessment, revealing complementary strengths; fused signals improve model accuracy via self-distillation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 图像美学评估主要采用成对偏好或逐点评分之一标注，两者互补性缺乏研究。本文构建PPaint双协议基准，在统一受控条件下收集专家对150幅中国画的多维度偏好与评分数据共45,900个成对判断。分析表明，偏好提供更一致的序数排名，评分则锚定绝对分数尺度；通过两种独立信号融合策略，能够训练出更准确且鲁棒的美学评估模型，并进一步通过自蒸馏将知识迁移到单协议模型，为图像质量评价的标注方法论提供了实证依据。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 图像美学评估主要采用成对偏好或逐点评分之一标注，两者互补性缺乏研究。本文构建PPaint双协议基准，在统一受控条件下收集专家对150幅中国画的多维度偏好与评分数据共45,900个成对判断。分析表明，偏好提供更一致的序数排名，评分则锚定绝对分数尺度；通过两种独立信号融合策略，能够训练出更准确且鲁棒的美学评估模型，并进一步通过自蒸馏将知识迁移到单协议模型，为图像质量评价的标注方法论提供了实证依据。

deep_dive.title: Preferences Order, Ratings Anchor: From Fused Expert Aesthetic Ground Truth to Self-Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: Pairwise preferences and pointwise ratings are the two dominant annotation protocols in image aesthetic assessment (IAA), yet existing benchmarks adopt only one, leaving their complementarity unmeasured under controlled conditions. We introduce PPaint, a matched dual-protocol benchmark in which 15 domain experts, 5 per category, annotate 150 Chinese paintings under both protocols across five aesthetic dimensions, collecting 45,900 pairwise expert judgments through a locally dense preference design alongside the matched ratings. The matched design reveals complementary strengths: preferences yield more consistent ordinal rankings, while ratings anchor the absolute score scale. Fusing both signals via two independent preference-to-score methods yields a fused expert ground truth on which the two constructions converge to nearly identical scores. deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PPaint benchmark collects both pairwise preferences and pointwise ratings for image aesthetic assessment, showing complementary strengths. A preference-rating fusion strategy improves aesthetic evaluation accuracy via self-distillation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图像美学评估中偏好与评分两种标注方式互补性不明的问题，构建双协议基准PPaint，同时收集15位专家对150幅国画的偏好与评分。发现偏好提供更一致的序数排名，而评分锚定绝对分值。提出偏好-评分融合自蒸馏框架，融合两种信号提升美学评估性能，为细粒度美学质量评价提供新范式。

deep_dive.title: SR-Ground: Image Quality Grounding for Super-Resolved Content
deep_dive.abstract: Super-Resolution (SR) has advanced rapidly in recent years, with diffusion-based models achieving unprecedented fidelity at the cost of introducing new types of visual artifacts. While existing Image Quality Assessment (IQA) methods provide holistic quality scores, they lack interpretability and fail to distinguish between different artifact types arising from modern SR approaches.   To address this gap, we introduce SR-Ground, a large-scale dataset specifically designed for fine-grained artifact segmentation in super-resolved images. The dataset comprises images processed by a diverse set of state-of-the-art SR models, with pixel-level annotations for multiple artifact categories. We conduct a large-scale crowdsourcing study involving 1,062 participants to validate and refine automatically generated segmentations, resulting in a high-quality dataset of 63,000 images spanning 6 distinct artifact types.   We demonstrate that training IQA models with grounding capabilities on SR-Ground significantly improves performance on downstream tasks. Furthermore, we introduce a fine-tuning pipeline that leverages our grounding model to reduce perceptible artifacts in SR outputs, showcasing the practical utility of our dataset.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces dataset for fine-grained artifact segmentation in super-resolved images to address lack of interpretability in IQA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 引入细粒度伪影分割数据集，解决图像质量评估缺乏可解释性的问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 引入细粒度伪影分割数据集，解决图像质量评估缺乏可解释性的问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 引入细粒度伪影分割数据集，解决图像质量评估缺乏可解释性的问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes SR-Ground, a dataset with pixel-level artifact annotations for super-resolved images, enabling interpretable image quality assessment by distinguishing artifact types, and validates it through a large crowdsourcing study.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有图像质量评估方法缺乏可解释性且无法区分超分辨率图像中不同伪影类型的问题，本文提出大规模数据集SR-Ground，包含多种SR模型生成的图像及其像素级伪影标注。通过1062名参与者的众包研究验证标注质量，并建立细粒度评估基准。该数据集有助于推动可解释的图像质量评价，实现从整体评分到局部伪影定位的跨越。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有图像质量评估方法缺乏可解释性且无法区分超分辨率图像中不同伪影类型的问题，本文提出大规模数据集SR-Ground，包含多种SR模型生成的图像及其像素级伪影标注。通过1062名参与者的众包研究验证标注质量，并建立细粒度评估基准。该数据集有助于推动可解释的图像质量评价，实现从整体评分到局部伪影定位的跨越。

deep_dive.title: Ultra-High-Definition Image Quality Assessment via Graph Representation Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Blind image quality assessment (BIQA) for ultrahighdefinition (UHD) images remains challenging because native-resolution inference is computationally expensive, whereas aggressive resizing or isolated cropping may suppress scale-sensitive distortions and weaken the relationship between local artifacts and global scene context. This paper aims to improve UHD-BIQA by explicitly modeling the structural dependencies among sampled image regions rather than treating them as independent views, and a graph representation learning framework UHD-GCN-BIQA is proposed. The framework samples aspect-ratio-aligned patches from each UHD image, encodes them as graph nodes, and constructs a hybrid k-nearest-neighbor graph using spatial proximity and feature similarity. Residual graph convolution is used to propagate contextual information across regions, and gated attention pooling aggregates patchlevel evidence into an imagelevel quality prediction. An exponential moving average normalized multiobjective loss function is adopted to stabilize the joint optimization of regression, correlation, and ranking objectives. Experiments on the UHD-IQA benchmark show that UHD-GCN-BIQA achieves PLCC = 0.7784, SRCC = 0.8019, and RMSE = 0.0519, obtaining competitive correlation performance and the lowest RMSE among the compared methods. These results indicate that graph-based region relation modeling is effective for UHD image quality assessment, particularly for improving absolute quality score estimation under high-resolution visual content.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: blind image quality assessment for ultra-high-definition images using graph learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用图学习的超高清图像盲质量评估
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用图学习的超高清图像盲质量评估
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用图学习的超高清图像盲质量评估
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UHD-GCN-BIQA uses graph convolutional networks to model structural dependencies among image patches for UHD blind IQA, capturing scale-sensitive distortions and global context better than independent patch methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对超高清图像盲质量评估中分辨率和局部-全局关系建模的难题，提出图表示学习框架UHD-GCN-BIQA。对图像块采样并构建混合k近邻图，通过图卷积建模空间与特征相似性中的结构依赖。在多个UHD数据库上验证，所提方法能有效捕捉尺度敏感失真，提升评估准确性，为高分辨率图像质量分析提供新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对超高清图像盲质量评估中分辨率和局部-全局关系建模的难题，提出图表示学习框架UHD-GCN-BIQA。对图像块采样并构建混合k近邻图，通过图卷积建模空间与特征相似性中的结构依赖。在多个UHD数据库上验证，所提方法能有效捕捉尺度敏感失真，提升评估准确性，为高分辨率图像质量分析提供新工具。

quick_skim.title: Pixel Perfect: Relational Image Quality Assessment with Spatially-Aware Distortions
quick_skim.abstract: Traditional image quality assessment (IQA) methods rely on mean opinion scores (MOS), which are resource-intensive to collect and fail to provide interpretable, localized feedback on specific image distortions. We overcome these limitations by shifting from absolute quality prediction to a relational and directional assessment. Our approach utilizes a self-supervised synthetic distortion engine to generate training data, eliminating the need for manual annotation. A distortion prediction network is trained with an anti-symmetric objective to produce spatially-aware, disentangled maps that identify the type, intensity, and direction of distortions relative to a reference image. Subsequently, a scoring network is trained via contrastive learning on ordinally ranked image sets to predict a relational quality score. Our method provides a more granular and interpretable approach to IQA for the targeted optimization of image processing algorithms without requiring any human-labeled quality scores.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: provides spatially-aware distortion maps as interpretable explanations for image quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供空间感知失真图作为图像质量的可解释反馈
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供空间感知失真图作为图像质量的可解释反馈
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供空间感知失真图作为图像质量的可解释反馈
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a relational IQA method that generates spatially-aware distortion maps for interpretable quality feedback, trained via self-supervised synthetic distortions and contrastive learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统图像质量评估缺乏可解释局部反馈的问题，提出一种关系型图像质量评估方法。利用自监督合成失真引擎生成训练数据，训练失真预测网络输出解耦的空间感知失真图，再通过对比学习训练评分网络。该方法能识别失真类型、强度和方向，提供比标量评分更丰富的质量信息，为图像质量评估带来可解释性提升。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对传统图像质量评估缺乏可解释局部反馈的问题，提出一种关系型图像质量评估方法。利用自监督合成失真引擎生成训练数据，训练失真预测网络输出解耦的空间感知失真图，再通过对比学习训练评分网络。该方法能识别失真类型、强度和方向，提供比标量评分更丰富的质量信息，为图像质量评估带来可解释性提升。

quick_skim.title: StableI2I: Spotting Unintended Changes in Image-to-Image Transition
quick_skim.abstract: In most real-world image-to-image (I2I) scenarios, existing evaluations primarily focus on instruction following and the perceptual quality or aesthetics of the generated images. However, they largely fail to assess whether the output image preserves the semantic correspondence and spatial structure of the input image. To address this limitation, we propose StableI2I, a unified and dynamic evaluation framework that explicitly measures content fidelity and pre--post consistency across a wide range of I2I tasks without requiring reference images, including image editing and image restoration. In addition, we construct StableI2I-Bench, a benchmark designed to systematically evaluate the accuracy of MLLMs on such fidelity and consistency assessment tasks. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that StableI2I provides accurate, fine-grained, and interpretable evaluations of content fidelity and consistency, with strong correlations to human subjective judgments. Our framework serves as a practical and reliable evaluation tool for diagnosing content consistency and benchmarking model performance in real-world I2I systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes an evaluation framework for image-to-image tasks that assesses content fidelity and consistency, using MLLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出用于图像到图像任务的评估框架，评估内容保真度和一致性，使用多模态大模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出用于图像到图像任务的评估框架，评估内容保真度和一致性，使用多模态大模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出用于图像到图像任务的评估框架，评估内容保真度和一致性，使用多模态大模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces StableI2I, a framework that evaluates image-to-image models on content fidelity and spatial consistency without reference images, and builds a benchmark to test MLLMs' accuracy on these assessments, advancing evaluation beyond perceptual quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有图像到图像转换的评估大多忽视输出图像与输入间的内容保真度和空间结构一致性。本文提出StableI2I框架，动态评估内容保真度和前后一致性，无需参考图像，覆盖图像编辑与复原等任务。同时构建基准测试多模态大模型在该评估任务上的准确性，推动生成图像质量评价从感知质量向结构化保真度拓展。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有图像到图像转换的评估大多忽视输出图像与输入间的内容保真度和空间结构一致性。本文提出StableI2I框架，动态评估内容保真度和前后一致性，无需参考图像，覆盖图像编辑与复原等任务。同时构建基准测试多模态大模型在该评估任务上的准确性，推动生成图像质量评价从感知质量向结构化保真度拓展。

quick_skim.title: ML-CLIPSim: Multi-Layer CLIP Similarity for Machine-Oriented Image Quality
quick_skim.abstract: We study full-reference image quality assessment from a machine-centric perspective, where images are evaluated by how well they preserve information for downstream models. We formulate machine-oriented quality as a latent machine utility and approximate it through pairwise predictive-consistency comparisons. To this end, we construct PCMP, a dataset of PSNR-matched distortion pairs labeled by consistency votes from multiple pretrained models. We further propose ML-CLIPSim, a differentiable quality metric built on a frozen CLIP visual encoder, which aggregates intermediate patch-token similarities and global image embeddings. Experiments on machine-preference benchmarks, human-IQA datasets, and learned image compression show that ML-CLIPSim better aligns with machine-oriented preferences than conventional fidelity and perceptual metrics, while remaining competitive for human quality prediction. Used as a compression distortion term, it improves rate--task trade-offs across multiple downstream tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于CLIP的机器导向全参考图像质量评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于CLIP的机器导向全参考图像质量评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于CLIP的机器导向全参考图像质量评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ML-CLIPSim is a machine-centric image quality metric using multi-layer CLIP features and predictive consistency comparisons.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文从机器视角研究全参考图像质量评估，将机器质量建模为潜在效用并通过成对一致性投票近似。提出ML-CLIPSim，基于冻结CLIP视觉编码器，融合中间层补丁-令牌相似度与全局嵌入，构建可微质量度量。实验表明该指标在机器偏好基准和人类IQA数据集上均优于现有方法，为面向机器任务的图像质量评估提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文从机器视角研究全参考图像质量评估，将机器质量建模为潜在效用并通过成对一致性投票近似。提出ML-CLIPSim，基于冻结CLIP视觉编码器，融合中间层补丁-令牌相似度与全局嵌入，构建可微质量度量。实验表明该指标在机器偏好基准和人类IQA数据集上均优于现有方法，为面向机器任务的图像质量评估提供了新工具。

quick_skim.title: Visual Aesthetic Benchmark: Can Frontier Models Judge Beauty?
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are now routinely deployed for visual understanding, generation, and curation. A substantial fraction of these applications require an explicit aesthetic judgment. Most existing solutions reduce this judgment to predicting a scalar score for a single image. We first ask whether such scores faithfully capture comparative preference: in a controlled study with eight expert annotators, score-derived rankings align poorly with the same annotators' direct comparisons, while direct ranking yields substantially higher inter-annotator agreement on best- and worst-image labels. Motivated by this finding, we introduce the Visual Aesthetic Benchmark (VAB), which casts aesthetic evaluation as comparative selection over candidate sets with matched subject matter. VAB contains 400 tasks and 1,195 images across fine art, photography, and illustration, with labels derived from the consensus of 10 independent expert judges per task. Evaluating 20 frontier MLLMs and six dedicated visual-quality reward models, we find that the strongest system identifies both the best and the worst image correctly across three random permutations of the candidate order in only 26.5% of tasks, far below the 68.9% achieved by human experts. Fine-tuning a 35B-parameter model on 2,000 expert examples brings its accuracy close to that of a 397B-parameter open-weight model, suggesting that the comparative signal in VAB is transferable. Together, these results expose a clear and measurable gap between current multimodal models and expert aesthetic judgment, and VAB provides the first set-based, expert-grounded testbed on which that gap can be tracked and closed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态大模型审美判断基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态大模型审美判断基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态大模型审美判断基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VAB evaluates MLLMs on comparative aesthetic judgment, finding that direct comparison outperforms scalar scoring.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法将审美判断简化为单张图像的标量评分，但研究发现标量评分排序与人工直接比较一致性差。本文提出Visual Aesthetic Benchmark (VAB)，将审美评估转化为候选集上的比较选择任务，并利用八名专家标注者收集数据。实验表明，通过直接比较排名可大幅提升标注一致性，VAB为评估和改进多模态大模型的视觉审美推理能力提供了有效平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有方法将审美判断简化为单张图像的标量评分，但研究发现标量评分排序与人工直接比较一致性差。本文提出Visual Aesthetic Benchmark (VAB)，将审美评估转化为候选集上的比较选择任务，并利用八名专家标注者收集数据。实验表明，通过直接比较排名可大幅提升标注一致性，VAB为评估和改进多模态大模型的视觉审美推理能力提供了有效平台。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: large language model that processes images and text

quick_skim.title: PROVE: A Perceptual RemOVal cohErence Benchmark for Visual Media
quick_skim.abstract: Evaluating object removal in images and videos remains challenging because the task is inherently one-to-many, yet existing metrics frequently disagree with human perception. Full-reference metrics reward copy-paste behaviors over genuine erasure; no-reference metrics suffer from systematic biases such as favoring blurry results; and global temporal metrics are insensitive to localized artifacts within edited regions. To address these limitations, we propose RC (Removal Coherence), a pair of perception-aligned metrics: RC-S, which measures spatial coherence via sliding-window feature comparison between masked and background regions, and RC-T, which measures temporal consistency via distribution tracking within shared restored regions across adjacent frames. To validate RC and support community benchmarking, we further introduce PROVE-Bench, a two-tier real-world benchmark comprising PROVE-M, an 80-video paired dataset with motion augmentation, and PROVE-H, a 100-video challenging subset without ground truth. Together, RC metrics and PROVE-Bench form the PROVE (Perceptual RemOVal cohErence) evaluation framework for visual media. Experiments across diverse image and video benchmarks demonstrate that RC achieves substantially stronger alignment with human judgments than existing evaluation protocols. The code for RC metrics and PROVE-Bench are publicly available at: https://github.com/xiaomi-research/prove/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes perceptual metrics for evaluating object removal quality in images
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出评估图像中物体移除质量的感知指标
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出评估图像中物体移除质量的感知指标
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出评估图像中物体移除质量的感知指标
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PROVE introduces Removal Coherence metrics (RC-S, RC-T) to evaluate object removal quality aligned with human perception, addressing biases in existing metrics. It provides a benchmark for spatial and temporal coherence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图像及视频物体移除评估中现有指标与人类感知不一致的问题，提出感知对齐的移除连贯性指标RC-S和RC-T。RC-S通过滑动窗口特征比较衡量空间连贯性，RC-T通过相邻帧分布跟踪衡量时间一致性。构建基准测试，验证指标更符合人类判断，为视觉媒体编辑质量评估提供可靠工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对图像及视频物体移除评估中现有指标与人类感知不一致的问题，提出感知对齐的移除连贯性指标RC-S和RC-T。RC-S通过滑动窗口特征比较衡量空间连贯性，RC-T通过相邻帧分布跟踪衡量时间一致性。构建基准测试，验证指标更符合人类判断，为视觉媒体编辑质量评估提供可靠工具。

quick_skim.title: VersusQ: Pairwise Margin Reasoning for Generalizable Video Quality Assessment
quick_skim.abstract: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) have shown promise for video quality assessment, but most methods still predict an absolute score for each video. Such pointwise supervision often mixes perceptual quality with dataset-specific calibration, including annotation protocols, rating habits, and score distributions. As a result, the learned scoring rule may work well within a benchmark but transfer poorly across unseen domains. We argue that relative comparisons alleviate the absolute-scale calibration bias by focusing purely on perceptual differences rather than dataset-specific rating habits. Consequently, we propose \textbf{VersusQ}, a pairwise margin reasoning framework driven entirely by direct comparisons. Specifically, VersusQ performs LMM-based comparison between two videos, reasons about their visual and temporal quality differences, and predicts a signed continuous margin that captures both the preferred choice and the degree of difference. Furthermore, to align interpretable comparison rationales with fine-grained numerical differences, we introduce Margin-Coupled GRPO, which jointly optimizes rollout-based relational reasoning and continuous margin regression. Extensive experiments on multiple public VQA benchmarks demonstrate that VersusQ achieves state-of-the-art performance, strong cross-domain generalization, and reliable fine-grained ranking under heterogeneous evaluation scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LMM-based pairwise margin reasoning for video quality assessment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于大语言多模态模型的成对边界推理视频质量评估

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于大语言多模态模型的成对边界推理视频质量评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: proposes VersusQ, a pairwise margin reasoning framework using LMMs for generalizable video quality assessment, avoiding absolute-score calibration bias.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统视频质量评估中绝对评分易受数据集特定标注习惯影响的问题，提出VersusQ框架，通过大语言多模态模型进行成对比较和边界推理，消除绝对量表偏差。该方法仅依赖相对感知差异，在未见域上展现出更强的泛化能力，为跨域视频质量评估提供了新范式。
